primary_complaint: Bilateral knee pain, right worse than left
functional_limitations: Cannot use stairs without hand rail
Pain limits ability to demonstrate drills as volunteer coach

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (02/15/2025): Right knee: Severe tricompartmental osteoarthritis with bone-on-bone medial compartment involvement, Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV, varus deformity 8 degrees. Left knee: Moderate OA, KL Grade III, less severe than right.

surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Anterior approach. Will perform standard primary TKA.

denial_risk_flags.label: Cortisone Injection Dates Undocumented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The two corticosteroid injections are dated only as 'approx 2023' with no exact dates, which may be insufficient for payer documentation requirements that mandate specific treatment dates to verify conservative care timeline.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain and document exact dates of the cortisone injections from the administering provider's records or pharmacy records to establish a precise conservative care timeline.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Payer Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 6 weeks of physical therapy is documented. Aetna PPO typically requires a minimum of 6–8 weeks of formal PT with documented failure; 6 weeks is at the lower boundary and may be challenged if outcomes documentation is sparse.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Provide detailed PT progress notes from Lakeside Rehab documenting specific functional outcomes, attendance, and clinical rationale for discontinuation to support the 6-week course as adequate and failed.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: PT at Lakeside Rehab for 6 weeks

denial_risk_flags.label: Hyaluronic Acid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The Synvisc-One injection dated 06/2024 lacks a documented follow-up period or duration of observation before concluding minimal benefit, which payers may require to confirm adequate trial.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart note specifying the follow-up interval after the 06/2024 Synvisc-One injection and the duration over which 'minimal benefit' was assessed.

denial_risk_flags.label: BMI 34.2 May Trigger Medical Review
denial_risk_flags.explanation: A BMI of 34.2 may prompt Aetna to request additional documentation of weight management counseling or a formal weight optimization program prior to elective TKA, as some payers apply BMI thresholds for surgical authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document any weight management counseling, dietary referral, or discussion of BMI optimization in the pre-operative notes to preempt a payer request for weight loss prior to surgery.

denial_risk_flags.label: Bilateral Diagnosis, Unilateral Request Noted
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart documents bilateral knee OA (M17.11 and M17.12) but requests only right TKA; payers may query whether the left knee diagnosis is relevant to the current authorization or flag it as a potential staged procedure requiring separate justification.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a brief note clarifying that M17.12 is documented for completeness but the current authorization request is limited to the right knee (M17.11) only, and that the left knee will be addressed separately if indicated.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: only requesting right side at this time

denial_risk_flags.label: ASA III Comorbidities Require Clearance Documentation
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The patient's ASA III classification with HTN, T2DM (A1c 7.1), and OSA on CPAP may require documented medical clearance from the patient's primary care or specialist prior to Aetna approving elective TKA.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Include pre-operative medical clearance documentation from the managing physician addressing cardiovascular risk, glycemic control (A1c 7.1), and OSA/CPAP compliance to satisfy Aetna's pre-surgical evaluation requirements.

diagnosis_codes.note: M17.11 (primary OA right knee) is a clinically appropriate and specific ICD-10 code that directly supports medical necessity for CPT 27447 right total knee arthroplasty.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: 1 of 2 duration-eligible treatments have an explicit numeric duration (50%). Meets the 50% threshold.

surgical_approach.note: The chart explicitly documents 'Anterior approach. Will perform standard primary TKA,' which is a specific, procedure-appropriate technique for CPT 27447.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27447 is a recognized orthopedic surgical code.
